title: GeoVersa: Spatial Intelligence Through Learned Anisotropic Kriging
description: GeoVersa implements ConvKrigingNet2D — a hybrid deep learning architecture for Digital Soil Mapping that fuses a 2D CNN raster-patch encoder, a tabular MLP, and a coordinate MLP through a differentiable anisotropic residual kriging layer. The kriging layer jointly learns the variogram range, anisotropy axes, and rotation from data gradients, and applies a distance-aware gate to suppress extrapolation noise under spatial cross-validation. Benchmarked against Random Forest using the Wadoux et al. (2021) design-based validation framework (n=500, Australia-wide soil organic carbon).
notes: Research preview. ConvKrigingNet2D architecture for Digital Soil Mapping. Benchmark uses the Wadoux et al. (2021) design-based validation framework.
